title: Money Matters with Matt: Markets at Highs (Still)
description: US markets have been breaking records this...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"US markets have been breaking records this year. By October, the S&P 500 had made more than two dozen new all-time highs, and the Nasdaq isn’t far behind. Closer to home, the JSE All Share Index is also trading near record levels, up just over 30 % over the past year.","html":"US markets have been breaking records this year. By October, the S&P 500 had made more than two dozen new all-time highs, and the Nasdaq isn’t far behind. Closer to home, the JSE All Share Index is also trading near record levels, up just over 30 % over the past year."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Whenever that happens, the same question comes up: Is now the time to take some money off the table?","html":"Whenever that happens, the same question comes up: Is now the time to take some money off the table?"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"It’s a fair question. A client messaged me recently asking exactly that, and I’ve been seeing the same headline everywhere: “Markets are at all-time highs … should investors be worried?”","html":"It’s a fair question. A client messaged me recently asking exactly that, and I’ve been seeing the same headline everywhere: “Markets are at all-time highs … should investors be worried?”"},{"type":"heading","text":"Highs are normal","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"A record high can sound alarming, but it’s actually the natural outcome of progress. If markets didn’t keep reaching new highs, investors wouldn’t earn long-term returns.","html":"A record high can sound alarming, but it’s actually the natural outcome of progress. If markets didn’t keep reaching new highs, investors wouldn’t earn long-term returns."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Last year the S&P 500 made 57 new highs; this year it’s already made 28. Over the past century, markets have spent roughly one-third of all trading days at record levels. That’s not a warning — that’s compounding doing its job.","html":"Last year the S&P 500 made 57 new highs; this year it’s already made 28. Over the past century, markets have spent roughly one-third of all trading days at record levels. That’s not a warning — that’s compounding doing its job."},{"type":"heading","text":"Valuations deserve perspective","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Yes, valuations are elevated. In the US, the Shiller CAPE ratio is close to 40 — levels last seen during the dot-com era. But valuation measures are poor short-term indicators. They can stay high for long stretches because markets are forward-looking: they price in expectations, not just current earnings.","html":"Yes, valuations are elevated. In the US, the Shiller CAPE ratio is close to 40 — levels last seen during the dot-com era. But valuation measures are poor short-term indicators. They can stay high for long stretches because markets are forward-looking: they price in expectations, not just current earnings."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Corporate results remain strong, profit margins healthy, and balance sheets robust. Optimism around technology and interest-rate cuts adds fuel to the story. Whether that fully justifies prices is open to debate, but it explains why investors are willing to pay up.","html":"Corporate results remain strong, profit margins healthy, and balance sheets robust. Optimism around technology and interest-rate cuts adds fuel to the story. Whether that fully justifies prices is open to debate, but it explains why investors are willing to pay up."},{"type":"heading","text":"The pull of cash","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When markets feel expensive, the instinct to “wait for a dip” is powerful. The problem is that it almost never works.","html":"When markets feel expensive, the instinct to “wait for a dip” is powerful. The problem is that it almost never works."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"To make it successful, you’d need to be right twice — once when you sell, and again when you buy back in. That second decision is the harder one. When prices fall 10 %, investors often wait for 20 %. When they fall 20 %, they expect 30 %. Few ever re-enter at the right time.","html":"To make it successful, you’d need to be right twice — once when you sell, and again when you buy back in. That second decision is the harder one. When prices fall 10 %, investors often wait for 20 %. When they fall 20 %, they expect 30 %. Few ever re-enter at the right time."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Meanwhile, earnings continue to grow, dividends reinvest, and the compounding you were counting on keeps working without you. Sitting in cash feels safe but comes at a hidden cost: missed participation.","html":"Meanwhile, earnings continue to grow, dividends reinvest, and the compounding you were counting on keeps working without you. Sitting in cash feels safe but comes at a hidden cost: missed participation."},{"type":"heading","text":"A better question","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Rather than asking whether to sell, ask whether your portfolio still matches your goals.","html":"Rather than asking whether to sell, ask whether your portfolio still matches your goals."},{"type":"list","items":["Do you need the money in the next 1–3 years? If yes, keep it in cash or short-term bonds.","Has your portfolio drifted from its target asset mix? If yes, rebalance.","Are you reacting to headlines or following your plan?"],"ordered":false},{"type":"paragraph","text":"All-time highs and uncertainty often appear together. That’s normal.","html":"All-time highs and uncertainty often appear together. That’s normal."},{"type":"heading","text":"Final thought","level":"h3"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Markets — both global and local — will always hand us reasons to hesitate. But investing isn’t about predicting the next pullback; it’s about staying invested for the next decade of growth.","html":"Markets — both global and local — will always hand us reasons to hesitate. But investing isn’t about predicting the next pullback; it’s about staying invested for the next decade of growth."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Valuations warrant respect, but patience and discipline deserve more.","html":"Valuations warrant respect, but patience and discipline deserve more."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Your edge isn’t in predicting. It’s in persevering — keeping your process stronger than your emotions.","html":"Your edge isn’t in predicting. It’s in persevering — keeping your process stronger than your emotions."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Matthew Matthee has a wealth management business that specialises in retirement planning and investments. He writes about financial markets, investments, and investor psychology. He holds a Masters Degree in Economics from Stellenbosch University and a Post Graduate Diploma in Financial Planning from UFS. [email protected]","html":"Matthew Matthee has a wealth management business that specialises in retirement planning and investments. He writes about financial markets, investments, and investor psychology. He holds a Masters Degree in Economics from Stellenbosch University and a Post Graduate Diploma in Financial Planning from UFS. [email protected]"},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]
